EX 32 – Item Barcode Automatic Validation Changes In many processes in WMOS, end users are scanning item barcodes. Base WMOS validates the barcode against its configured barcode as well as the item’s cross references. In some cases, items will share cross references and by extension have a shared value that is accepted as two different item’s barcode. In certain flows where this shared barcode is input in a scan, WMOS will prompt the user to confirm which of the two items they are intending. In flows where this can be inferred from some relevant detail that the system has available to it, EX32 will customize WMOS behavior to assume the item which is most likely the input from this information. WMOS will suppress the prompt asking the user to choose an item. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF N Reporting N Other N Assumptions T his extension modifies behavior only in three flows: Receiving, Cycle Counting , oLPN Audit . Receiving will compare against the ASN/Shipment. Cycle Counting will compare against the Location oLPN Audit will compare against the oLPN/ Item If WMOS cannot find relevant details in one of the above listed ways, WM will still prompt the user to confirm which item they have scanned. If the ASN/Shipment has both items present, WMOS will still confirm with the user which item has been scanned. If the location being cycle counted has both items assigned, WMOS will still confirm with the user which item has been scanned. Manual cycle count will share this behavior and validate against the location which has been scanned and the inventory which is expected to be there in WM_INVENTORY. Parameter 25-25 “Allow receipt of SKU not on shipment” on Receiving transactions is configured to “No” Dependencies Code Dependencies N/A Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Proces s Flow Diagram Process Steps UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. MHE Outputs Activity Tracking PIX No changes to base behavior for PIX generation as part of this extension. LM Hooks Not in scope as part of this extension. RF RF Receiving Program Platform   Available   Text Based (RF Gun)  Y  UI  N  Mobile (DM)  N  During base receiving, if a user scans an item barcode that is shared with another ite m ’s XREFs , WM prompts the user to confirm which of the two items the user is scanning. Base RF Receiving is enhanced to suppress the item selection screen and update the item to be selected when the item barcode is scanned. When the item barcode is scanned, WM check s the ASN detail and item master table to fetch the item present o n the ASN of the shipment being received. If both items that share the item barcode are present o n the ASN of the shipment bring received , then prompt for the item selection screen. Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code ID 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code ID EX32 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX32 is turned off, and no custom EX32 processing will occur. Navigation – RF Receiving - Suppress Item Selection Prompt Display The following screen shot is the item selection screen that is suppressed after scanning an item barcode if the scanned item barcode is shared with other items. Validations WMOS first validates if parameter 25-25 “Allow receipt of SKU not on shipment” is configured to “No” . If this validation passes, EX32 is enhanced to check the ASN Detail and Item master table to fetch the item present o n the ASN after the item barcode scan during receiving . This eliminates the need for the item selection screen above as WMOS know s the exact item being received . If two items that share the item barcode scanned are on the ASN of the shipment being received , WMOS display s the item selection screen above . If parameter 25-25 “Allow receipt of SKU not on shipment” is configured to “YES”, follow the base flow. Updates Per base Out puts Activity Tracking No changes to base Activity Tracking as part of this extension. PIX No changes to base PIX as part of this extension. LM Hooks No changes to base labor hooks as part of this extension. RF Cycle Count Program Platform   Available   Text Based (RF Gun)  Y  UI  N  Mobile (DM)  N  Per b ase C ycle C ount , if a user scans an item barcode that is shared with another item ’s XREFs , WM prompts the user to confirm which of the two items the user is scanning. With EX32, b ase RF Cycle Count is enhanced to suppress the item selection screen and at the time of item barcode scan , validate the item barcode sca n n ed against the item in the scanned location . During RF Cycle Count, u ser scans the location first and then the item barcode. When the item barcode is scanned , WM checks the WM_INVENTORY table and cross references the location scanned with the item barcode scanned to determine which item to take as an input if the item barcode scanned is shared across multiple items . This eliminates the reason for the item selection screen as WM will know which item the user is scanning. If the item barcode scanned is not present in the location scanned but shares the item barcode with another item’s XREF s , display the item selection screen to have the user select the item. Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code ID 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code ID EX32 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX32 is turned off, and no custom EX32 processing will occur. Navigation – RF Cycle Count - Suppress Item Selection Prompt Display The following screen shot is the item selection screen that is suppressed after scanning an item barcode if the scanned item barcode is shared with other items. Validations Updates Per base Outputs Activity Tracking No changes to base Activity Tracking as part of this extension. PIX No changes to base PIX as part of this extension. LM Hooks No changes to base labor hooks as part of this extension. RF oLPN Audit Program Platform   Available   Text Based (RF Gun)  Y  UI  N  Mobile (DM)  N  During RF oLPN Audit , if a user scans an item barcode that is shared with another item ’s XREF s , WM prompts the user to confirm which of the two items the user is scanning. Base RF oLPN Audit is enhanced to suppress the item selection screen and at the time of item barcode scan, check the LPN _DETAIL and ITEM_CBO table to fetch the item present in the oLPN being audited. User scans the oLPN to audit first and then the item barcode. When the item barcode is scanned, WM validates it against the oLPN scanned and check the LPN Detail table to know exactly which item is scanned . This eliminates the need to have the item selection prompt since WMOS will know which item from checking the LPN and the LPN detail table. If the item barcode scanned is not present in the LPN scanned but shares the item barcode with another item’s XREF s , display the item selection screen to have the user select the item. Enablement The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code ID 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code ID EX32 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX32 is turned off, and no custom EX32 processing will occur. Navigation – RF oLPN Audit - Suppress Item Selection Prompt Display The following screen shot is the item selection screen that is suppressed after scanning an item barcode if the scanned item barcode is shared with other item. Validations Updates Per base Outputs Activity Tracking No changes to base Activity Tracking as part of this extension. PIX No changes to base PIX as part of this extension LM Hooks Process Change/Addition No additional process changes in scope for this extension. Reporting , Label, or Document Not report, label or document changes in scope for this extension. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Performance Expectations The p urpose of this section is to identify performance requirements up front Process Step Peak Transaction Rate Max Response 95% Response Recovery Queue Recovery Time Operations Driver & notes Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below.
